- Success and failure invariants for [13] Success criterion: continuous lockstep validation are explicitly quantified and machine-verifiable.
- Determinism requirements for [13] Success criterion: continuous lockstep validation are validated across repeated runs and adversarial perturbations.


Expected Artifacts:
- Machine-readable verification artifact with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_13/bd-1w78/verification_evidence.json, suitable for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_13/bd-1w78/verification_summary.md, linking implementation intent to measured validation outcomes.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- The implementation must deliver the exact capability named in the title, with no scope dilution and no silent feature omission.
- Validation artifacts must include capability-specific thresholds, pass/fail criteria, and reproducible evidence bundles tied to this bead ID.
- Program/section verification gates must be able to consume this bead's outputs without manual interpretation.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[13] Success criterion: continuous lockstep validation" is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.

- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Lockstep validation runs continuously: every CI pipeline execution includes a lockstep comparison against reference Node.js on a representative test subset.
2. The representative subset covers >= 100 API calls across critical API families (fs, http, crypto, stream, net).
3. Any lockstep divergence fails CI and produces a divergence receipt with: input, expected output, actual output, API family, severity.
4. Lockstep validation history is tracked: a dashboard or report shows pass/fail trend over the last 30 days.
5. Divergence receipts are immutable once created (append-only storage).
6. Lockstep validation can be triggered on-demand for a specific API family (not just full suite).
7.  {"id":"bd-1xao","title":"[13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 13

Task Objective:
Track and gate production-grade adoption of impossible-by-default capabilities.

Execution Requirements:
- Make this requirement machine-verifiable and release-gated where applicable.
- Keep behavior self-documenting so future contributors do not need to re-open the master plan.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for rule/contract logic and error conditions.
- Integration/E2E tests for workflow-level enforcement.
- Structured logs with stable codes and trace IDs.
- Reproducible artifacts that prove contract satisfaction.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Success and failure invariants for [13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption are explicitly quantified and machine-verifiable.
- Determinism requirements for [13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption are validated across repeated runs and adversarial perturbations.


Expected Artifacts:
- Machine-readable verification artifact with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_13/bd-1xao/verification_evidence.json, suitable for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_13/bd-1xao/verification_summary.md, linking implementation intent to measured validation outcomes.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- The implementation must deliver the exact capability named in the title, with no scope dilution and no silent feature omission.
- Validation artifacts must include capability-specific thresholds, pass/fail criteria, and reproducible evidence bundles tied to this bead ID.
- Program/section verification gates must be able to consume this bead's outputs without manual interpretation.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption" is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption" prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"1. 'Impossible-by-default' capabilities are defined: operations that are blocked unless explicitly enabled with cryptographic authorization.
2. At minimum, these capabilities are impossible-by-default: (a) arbitrary file system access outside project root, (b) outbound network to non-allowlisted hosts, (c) spawning child processes without sandbox, (d) loading unsigned extensions, (e) disabling hardening profiles.
3. Each impossible-by-default capability requires explicit opt-in via signed capability token with expiry.
4. Attempting a blocked operation produces a clear, actionable error message (not a generic permission denied).
5. Adoption metric: >= 90% of production deployments run with all impossible-by-default capabilities enforced (measured via telemetry).

 {"id":"bd-3tpg","title":"[10.15] Enforce canonical all-point cancellation injection gate (from `10.14`) for critical control workflows.","description":"## Why This Exists
Hard Runtime Invariant #3 (cancellation protocol semantics) cannot be trusted unless cancellation is injected at every possible suspension point and the system is verified to maintain its invariants (no obligation leaks, no half-commit outcomes, no quiescence violations). Section 10.14 (bd-876n) built the canonical all-point cancellation injection framework that inserts cancellation signals at every await point in a workflow. This bead enforces that canonical injection gate on franken_node's critical control workflows, making it a CI requirement that every high-impact protocol survives cancellation at every await point without violating any runtime invariant.

## What This Must Do
1. Implement `tests/lab/control_cancellation_injection.rs` that:
   - Uses the canonical 10.14 cancellation injection framework (bd-876n).
   - For each critical control workflow (lifecycle, rollout, quarantine, migration, fencing, health-gate), runs the workflow and injects cancellation at every await point (one run per injection point).
   - After each injection, asserts: (a) no obligation leaks (all obligations committed or rolled back), (b) no half-commit outcomes (state is either fully committed or fully rolled back), (c) no quiescence violations (all regions are closed, all child tasks drained).
   - Collects results into a structured report.
2. Generate `artifacts/10.15/control_cancel_injection_report.json` with:
   - Per-workflow, per-injection-point results: injection_point (await site identifier), obligations_status, commit_status, quiescence_status, pass/fail.
   - Summary: total injection points, total passes, total failures, first failure details.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Canonical cancellation injection runs on every critical protocol flow; no obligation leaks, no half-commit outcomes, no quiescence violations.
- The injection gate uses the canonical 10.14 framework, not custom injection logic.
- Every await point in every critical workflow is covered (not just a sample).
- A single failure at any injection point fails the gate.
- The injection report is consumed by the section gate (bd-20eg).

## Testing & Logging Requirements
- **Unit tests**: Validate the injection harness setup with a trivial workflow (one await point, cancel there, assert rollback).
- **Integration tests**: Full injection sweep on lifecycle workflow. Full sweep on rollout workflow.
- **Conformance tests**: Assert the injection framework covers all await points (compare against AST analysis of await points in the workflow).
- **Adversarial tests**: Introduce a workflow with a hidden await point (e.g., inside a utility function); assert the injection framework still finds it. Introduce a workflow that leaks an obligation on cancel; assert the gate catches it.
- **Structured logs**: Event codes `CIN-001` (injection point enumerated), `CIN-002` (cancellation injected), `CIN-003` (post-cancel assertion passed), `CIN-004` (post-cancel assertion failed — obligation leak), `CIN-005` (post-cancel assertion failed — half-commit), `CIN-006` (post-cancel assertion failed — quiescence violation). Include workflow_id, injection_point_id, and trace correlation ID.

## Expected Artifacts
- `tests/lab/control_cancellation_injection.rs`
- `artifacts/10.15/control_cancel_injection_report.json`
- `artifacts/section_10_15/bd-3tpg/verification_evidence.json`
- `artifacts/section_10_15/bd-3tpg/verification_summary.md`

## Dependencies
- **Upstream**: bd-876n (10.14 — canonical all-point cancellation injection framework)
- **Downstream**: bd-20eg (section gate)","acceptance_criteria":"- Canonical cancellation injection runs on every critical protocol flow; no obligation leaks, no half-commit outcomes, no quiescence violations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:37:00.790939581Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T02:10:56.276714909Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T02:10:56.276684432Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-15","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3tpg","depends_on_id":"bd-876n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T14:59:37.895649831Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+{"id":"bd-3tw7","title":"[replacement-gap] Add replacement-critical truthfulness gate for migration/policy/runtime/verifier/extension/control surfaces","description":"Why this exists:
Multiple closed sections currently still have replacement-critical product paths with placeholder or surrogate trust logic. The deeper audit expanded the affected surface beyond migration/policy/runtime/verifier paths: extension admission, trust-card materialization, certification, control-channel auth, and product session-auth still contain security-critical shortcuts that can make the tracker and release story look green while the underlying trust claims are not actually true.

What this bead must do:
Create a replacement-critical truthfulness gate covering migration autopilot/artifacts, compatibility authorization, safe mode, supervision time/budget logic, anti-entropy reconciliation, verifier SDK/economy surfaces, extension registry admission, trust-card/certification derivation, control-channel auth, and product session-auth. The gate should combine static scanning, dynamic adversarial witness corpora, and deterministic evidence emission. It must explicitly block the program success narrative if any critical path still relies on fabricated hashes, signature-present shortcuts, fake duration math, clock stubs, proof-shape checks, decorative signed-looking strings, token-present auth, or asserted trust inputs that bypass verified evidence.

Acceptance criteria:
1. The gate statically scans replacement-critical non-engine surfaces including `migration_pipeline.rs`, `migration_artifact.rs`, `compat_gates.rs`, `compatibility_gate.rs`, `safe_mode.rs`, `supervision.rs`, `anti_entropy.rs`, `verifier_economy/mod.rs`, `connector/verifier_sdk.rs`, `supply_chain/extension_registry.rs`, `supply_chain/trust_card.rs`, `supply_chain/certification.rs`, `connector/control_channel.rs`, `api/session_auth.rs`, plus directly related helpers for banned placeholder markers and trust surrogates.
2. The gate runs a dynamic witness corpus including: forged compatibility receipts, migration inputs whose names previously spoofed heuristics, corrupted/stale trust-state scenarios, burst restart schedules, zero-filled/fake proofs, replay capsules with decorative signatures, forged extension manifests, attestation-chain truncations, caller-injected fake trust-card inputs, guessed control tokens, and decorative session signatures.
3. Any surviving placeholder/surrogate acceptance path fails the gate closed and produces a machine-readable gap report mapping witness -> offending code path -> remediation bead.
4. The gate is wired into replacement-relevant verification so section/program completion cannot stay green while these trust surrogates survive.
5. The gate is deterministic, reproducible, and cheap enough to run in CI on every claim-critical change.
6. Deliver the checker/tests plus JSON and Markdown evidence artifacts with stable schemas and trace IDs.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The truthfulness gate closes only after the root-cause bug beads and the relevant section-level verification gates for 10.0, 10.3, 10.4, 10.5, 10.8, 10.10, 10.11, 10.13, 10.17, and 15 are all green, so no cross-surface replacement claim can bypass comprehensive local unit, integration, and E2E evidence.
2. Static scanning must cover banned placeholder markers, proof-shape shortcuts, decorative signature handling, asserted trust inputs, token-present auth, fake duration math, and related surrogate patterns across all named replacement-critical files plus directly related helpers.
3. Dynamic witness packs must cover at minimum: forged migration receipts, degraded evidence sources, forged compatibility receipts, stale safe-mode frontiers, supervision restart storms, anti-entropy fork or partition cases, forged replay capsules, forged extension manifests, caller-injected trust inputs, guessed control tokens, and decorative session signatures.
4. Gate output must be useful to humans: failures emit stable reason codes, remediation bead mappings, a Markdown summary, and a machine-readable report that ties each failing witness to the exact code path and evidence bundle.
5. At least one deterministic operator E2E shell script under tests/e2e/ runs the truthfulness gate end to end, captures detailed logs for every witness family, and proves deterministic replay on repeated runs.
6. Structured logs expose stable TRUTHFULNESS_GATE_* event families with required fields: trace_id, witness_id, surface, offending_path, decision, reason_code, remediation_bead, and evidence_bundle_id.
7. Evidence artifacts under artifacts/replacement_gap/bd-3tw7/ include verification_evidence.json, verification_summary.md, witness_matrix.json, and any rch offload build IDs used for gate execution or benchmark collection.
8. CI integration must fail closed on missing artifacts, stale artifacts, nondeterministic results, or witness regressions, and the total gate runtime must stay within an explicit CI budget justified by
